response:
## The three IA drifts

| # | Finding | ID | agent_risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Permission drift on `/admin` (admin / manager / owner across nav, guard, docs) | crime_00003 | **0.59** |
| 2 | Docs reference `iaq teams/workspace/refresh`; bin only advertises `get, list` | crime_00006 | 0.51 |
| 3 | Parallel destination: `/admin/billing-plans` mirrors `/billing-plans` | crime_00004 | 0.48 |

## Recommendations

**1. Permission IA Drift → BLOCK MERGE.**
Three different role tokens (`admin`, `manager`, `owner`) describe the same destination across `src/nav.ts:6`, `src/routes/admin.ts` (which is itself internally inconsistent — uses `admin` at line 2, `manager` at lines 2 and 9, `owner` at line 4), and `docs/routes.md`. There is no single source of truth for who can reach `/admin`. `test_gap` is 1.0, so nothing catches a drift when an agent edits one surface. An agent asked to "tighten admin access" or "let managers in" will pick one token, leave the others, and silently produce either a privilege escalation or a lockout. Pick the canonical token and align all three before merge.

**2. Docs Reference Missing Command → WARN.**
`docs/routes.md:23,27,31` reference `iaq teams / workspace / refresh`; the bin only advertises `get, list`. Confidence 0.8 but blast radius is bounded — users hit `command not found` and an agent following the docs to build a fix will fail loudly rather than silently. Fix in the same PR if practical, otherwise file and merge.

**3. Parallel Destination → WARN.**
`/admin/billing-plans` and `/billing-plans` overlap with no nav/doc/import link between them. Real bifurcation, but both routes are also orphaned (crime_00007 and crime_00008) — neither is reachable from nav or docs, so user-facing blast radius is currently zero. Demands a decision (canonical + redirect, or document the split) before either route is wired up, but doesn't have to gate this PR unless this PR is the one wiring them up.

## Highest agent risk: Permission IA Drift (crime_00003)

Not just because the score is highest (0.59 vs 0.51 / 0.48), but because of the *failure mode*:

- **Three sources of truth on an auth boundary.** Nav, route guard, and docs each describe `/admin` access differently. An agent extending one will leave the others stale.
- **The route guard is internally inconsistent.** `src/routes/admin.ts` itself references all three tokens in five places. Even a careful agent reading just that file gets contradictory signal.
- **`test_gap: 1.0`.** Nothing catches the drift. Compare to the docs-command drift, which fails loudly at runtime.
- **Silent-failure shape.** A wrong-token edit either lets the wrong role in (security incident) or locks the right one out (visible but degrading). Neither is caught by typecheck/tests.

The other two are *correctness/findability* problems. This one is an *authorization* problem with no test backstop — that's the combination that turns confident-but-wrong agent edits into incidents.
